citations.narrative:
In the enclosure housing one rabbit, there is an area beneath the raised bed that is not being cleaned enough. In this area
of the enclosure, the wire floor has trapped old hair and dried feces leading to an accumulation of debris.
Failure to remove accumulations of excreta or other debris from primary enclosures can allow for the spread of disease
hazards, create unpleasant odors, and attract pests. Primary enclosures for rabbits shall be kept reasonably free of
excreta, hair, cobwebs, and other debris by periodic cleaning.
To be corrected by February 17, 2026.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
In the side yard beside the owl enclosure, there is an excessive accumulation of weeds, leaves, and other vegetation.
Within this overgrown area, there are broken plastic bins and other discarded materials.
Accumulations of vegetation and debris can attract pests and pose a health risk to the animals. Premises where housing
facilities are located must be kept clean and in good repair in order to protect the birds from injury and disease, to
facilitate husbandry practices required in this subpart, and to reduce or eliminate breeding and living areas for rodents,
pests, and vermin. Premises must be kept free of accumulations of trash, junk, waste products, and discarded matter.
Weeds, grasses, and bushes must be controlled so as to facilitate cleaning of the premises and pest control, and to
protect the health and wellbeing of the birds.
To be corrected by February 17, 2025.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative and the licensee.
n

narrative:
3.56(a)(1) 
   
Sanitation. 
In the enclosure housing one rabbit, there is an area beneath the raised bed that is not being cleaned enough. In this area 
of the enclosure, the wire floor has trapped old hair and dried feces leading to an accumulation of debris.  
Failure to remove accumulations of excreta or other debris from primary enclosures can allow for the spread of disease 
hazards, create unpleasant odors, and attract pests. Primary enclosures for rabbits shall be kept reasonably free of 
excreta, hair, cobwebs, and other debris by periodic cleaning. 
To be corrected by February 17, 2026. 
3.158(c) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
In the side yard beside the owl enclosure, there is an excessive accumulation of weeds, leaves, and other vegetation. 
Within this overgrown area, there are broken plastic bins and other discarded materials.  
Accumulations of vegetation and debris can attract pests and pose a health risk to the animals. Premises where housing 
facilities are located must be kept clean and in good repair in order to protect the birds from injury and disease, to 
facilitate husbandry practices required in this subpart, and to reduce or eliminate breeding and living areas for rodents, 
pests, and vermin. Premises must be kept free of accumulations of trash, junk, waste products, and discarded matter. 
Weeds, grasses, and bushes must be controlled so as to facilitate cleaning of the premises and pest control, and to 
protect the health and wellbeing of the birds. 
To be corrected by February 17, 2025. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative and the licensee.
